text.title: go-concurrency Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-concurrency as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Generator goroutine without a ctx.Done() exit: go func() { for i := 0; ; i++ { ch <- i } }()
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If no consumer reads from ch, the goroutine blocks forever. Memory and goroutine count grow unboundedly.

failure-mode.description: Concurrent write to a plain map without a mutex: go func() { cache[key]++ }()
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Concurrent map access is undefined behaviour in Go. The race detector catches it, but it may silently corrupt data or panic in production.

failure-mode.description: Calling wg.Add(1) inside the goroutine body: go func() { wg.Add(1); defer wg.Done(); ... }()
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The goroutine may not execute before wg.Wait() is called if the scheduler yields, causing premature completion.

failure-mode.description: Closing a channel from a goroutine other than the writer: producer sends on ch, but a separate cleanup goroutine closes(ch). If the producer is blocked on send, the receiver gets a panic.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Closing a send-on-closed-channel causes panic. Only the sender can close. If cleanup needs to stop the producer, use a separate done channel: close(done), and the producer selects on done to exit.

failure-mode.description: Unbuffered channel without a receiver: ch := make(chan int); go func() { ch <- 1; ch <- 2 }() (but main never reads from ch). The goroutine blocks forever on the first send.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: An unbuffered send blocks until there is a receiver. If no goroutine is receiving, the sender leaks. Use a buffered channel if the producer outpaces consumers, or ensure a receiver drains the channel.
